evidence: deltas derived from shadow prices + counterfactual outputs; plausible signs/magnitudes
8 scenarios but outcomes monotonically ordered; little site-specific reordering
SS bounded [0.341,0.713]; baseline S0=0.5 reference; lower=better

Mathematically EXPECTED not a bug: S7='best-case stabilized' has all-improving deltas (delay/risk/alert reduced) -> always lowest SS; S6='worst-case drift' has all-worsening deltas -> always highest SS. By construction these bound the set, so argmin=S7 argmax=S6 for every site deterministically.
ranking/plan_comparison therefore tautological; do not help operators choose among realistic plans
override/confidence SS components near-constant -> SS driven by delay+risk only
S7 assumes full effectiveness gain and S6 assumes uniform drift escalation; idealized bounds not operational plans
TCS recursively includes DCS plus its own components (double counting)
twin presented as scenario ranking; better framed as sensitivity bounds given deterministic S7/S6
